headline: D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) Posts Q2 CY2026 Sales In Line With Estimates But Full-Year Sales Guidance Misses Expectations Significantly
summary: Homebuilder D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, but sales were flat year on year at $9.23 billion. On the other hand, the company’s full-year revenue guidance of $32.75 billion at the midpoint came in 3.1% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $3.20 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

On the other hand, the company’s full-year revenue guidance of $32.75 billion at the midpoint came in 3.1% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $3.20 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) Reports Mixed Q3 With Earnings Beat and Cautious Outlook
summary: D.R. Horton (DHI) beat Q3 earnings estimates with $3.20 EPS, though revenue of $9.23B narrowly missed. Net income fell 12% due to higher cancellations and incentives, with cautious full-year guidance sending stock down 0.38% pre-market.

headline: D.R. Horton up in premarket after Q3 earnings beat despite weak outlook
